A solemn, drowned shrine-hall steeped in funereal silence, low and wide beneath weeping vaults, where hooded ritual and the taste of cold seawater hang heavy in the air and everything glistens with brine and dim, pooling candlelight. The floor is uneven salt-stained flagstone, slabs slick with brine and grouted in black silt, crusted at their edges with pale barnacles and skinned with a thin sheet of standing tidewater that mirrors every flame, while overhead the low groined vaults of brick and mortar hang furred with mineral drip-stone and trailing strands of dead kelp, weeping slow beads of seawater. The far wall of dressed basalt is carved with concentric tide-rings and streaked white with salt rime, water trickling down its veins, and the flanking walls are honeycombed with shallow burial niches - the left hung with rotted votive ribbons and crusted barnacle clusters, the right salt-bleached and scrawled with the cult's looping chalk-and-weed tide-script. At the center of the far wall a carved stone relief of the drowned tide-saint gazes out hollow-eyed and crowned in coral, water seeping endlessly from its open mouth; below it in the center background rises a great slab altar of black basalt streaked with salt, set on shallow worn steps and cupped with channels that drain runoff brine back to the floor, and upon it sits a wide verdigris-green bronze basin brimming with black brine. Framing the altar in cold flame, a tall candelabrum of fused coral and yellowed bone stands in the left background with its twin at the right, both bearing guttering tallow candles whose wax sheets down them like melt over reef. In the left midground a low stone bier bears a shrouded corpse bound in kelp and weighted with stones, its linen darkened with seep, mirrored by a matching bier in the right midground laid with another shrouded form awaiting the tides, and between them at the center a woven kelp-fiber prayer-mat lies spread flat across the flagstones, salt-stiffened and worn pale where countless knees have pressed. In the left foreground clusters a set of barnacled clay grave-urns holding the salt-ash and small tokens of the long-drowned, and in the right foreground a slender lectern grown of pale coral holds open a swollen, water-buckled book of tide-litanies, while overhead to the right a censer of green glass and coral hangs on a corroded chain, smoking faintly with salt-incense and dripping a slow thread of condensation. Atmospheric and highly detailed dark-fantasy realism, candlelit chiaroscuro, damp volumetric haze, intricate wet textures of salt, brine, coral, and bone, muted basalt-and-verdigris palette, painterly and cinematic.
